title: Would you use an AI that grades your trading setup before entering a trade?
author: StrangerDesperate360
selftext: I'm working on a small project for traders and would love some honest feedback. The idea is simple: Before entering a trade, you upload a chart screenshot and briefly explain your setup. The AI then gives: \* a setup grade (A+ to D) \* strengths and weaknesses \* risk assessment \* suggestions for improvement The goal is not signals or trade predictions. The goal is helping traders avoid low-quality setups and improve consistency. Would you actually use something like this before taking a trade? What would make it valuable for you?

comments.author: Careless-Diamond-277
comments.body: No dont do It Like That automate it into a bot or Addon so bot will automaticly do what you Plan to do

comments.author: jpeace116
comments.body:
I've built something like that for stock/index options. I think it's good for exploration but obviously grading can become extremely subjective according to trade philosophy and personal preferred strategies. Mines uses market data API (paid subscription) to grab options chains and to back test. I also have an engine that silently generates trades, grades them, tracks PnL, regime, and other things to then calibrate how it structures and grades future trades. Only been about a week so we'll see how it goes. The goal is to connect it to a broker via API so the app can submit any hand-picked trades to the broker directly and track the position via a portfolio advisor. 

Initially used Claude to build but now also use Codex via CLI so that Claude can plan changes then call Codex via CLI to code. If nothing else, it's at least a fun side-project while I continue to trade manually.

comments.author: PleasantSomewhere990
comments.body: The hard part is calibrating the grade against real outcomes, not just making the chart read clean

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body:
100%.

However you have to be careful as in a single chart is not enough for the AI to truly analyze. A chart has only one dimension and limited amount of info it can hold. 

Look into CMT theory and see if you can feed it more data. It will help more. And also it will need multi time frame as well. 

Best of luck!
